evals.prompt: We forked CoPaw from agentscope-ai/CoPaw and ship our own release. I want to merge upstream main into our local main but I have never set upstream. Walk me through the exact git commands and what to do if merge conflicts appear in src/Dockerfile.
evals.expected_output: Adds upstream remote, fetches, merges upstream/<default-branch>; for src/Dockerfile (outside src/copaw/console/next-console) prefer keeping local version per directory scope, do not adopt upstream packaging blindly; then verify only scoped dirs for product integration

evals.prompt: Upstream PR 42 fixes a bug we need, but it is still open. I only want those commits on our branch `local`, not a full upstream merge. How do I fetch that PR and cherry-pick safely?
evals.expected_output: Uses git fetch upstream pull/42/head, inspects log, cherry-picks onto a topic branch from local, mentions gh pr view as optional

evals.prompt: After cherry-picking three commits from upstream, `git cherry-pick` stopped with conflicts in next-console/package.json. I do not want to lose our pnpm overrides. What should I check before continuing the cherry-pick?
evals.expected_output: Open conflicted file, preserve downstream overrides while taking upstream dependency changes intentionally, git add and cherry-pick --continue, or abort path

evals.prompt: Upstream added a new FastAPI route for feature X. We already have something similar in our fork. What should we do before merging?
evals.expected_output: Compare behavior with Integration principles: skip duplicate and tell user if equivalent; otherwise understand intent, check next-console usage, verify multi-user isolation and auth, full-stack update if needed

evals.prompt: We merged upstream main with no conflicts. Can we ship immediately?
evals.expected_output: No: merging is transport only; still read upstream changes, refactor into local codebase if needed, full-stack and multi-user verification before ship

evals.prompt: Upstream main has 5 commits; 4 only touch .github/workflows and README.md, 1 fixes a bug in src/copaw/foo.py. What should we take?
evals.expected_output: Focus on src/ change; skip or ignore workflow and README upstream updates per directory convention unless user explicitly wants them; port foo.py under integration principles
